name: Wisconsin Physical Therapist License
description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wisconsin - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Wisconsin DSPS — Physical Therapist
provider.parentOrganization: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)

address.description: Department of Safety and Professional Services, P.O. Box 8368, Madison, WI 53708-8368

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Wisconsin physical therapist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $135. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.
Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for physical therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Wisconsin participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Wisconsin without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Wisconsin?

acceptedAnswer.text: Current board processing time is 3 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
